title: Sersi
content:
{{CharHub|Sersi|Sersi}} {{Character |image = Sersi Character Poster.jpg |real name = Sersi |alias = {{Alias |nicknames = The Matter Manipulator{{Ref|E5YWIC1}} |undercover = Sylvia{{Ref|E}}}} |species = [[Eternal]] |citizenship = {{Citizenship|GRBR}}
{{Citizenship|ENG}} |gender = Female |DOB = |snapped = No{{Ref|EternalsSnap}} |title = Prime Eternal |affiliation = [[Natural History Museum]] |movie = ''[[Eternals (film)|Eternals]]'' |tv series = {{Infobox List|{{Collapse|''[[She-Hulk: Attorney at Law]]'' (photo)}}}} |comic = ''[[Eternals: 500-Year War Infinity Comic]]'' |web series = ''[[The Untold Tales of the Eternals]]'' (2 episodes) |actor = [[Gemma Chan]] |status = Alive}} {{Quote|This [[Earth|planet]]'s my home, but I can't pretend to be [[human]] anymore.|Sersi to [[Dane Whitman]]|Eternals (film)}} '''Sersi''' is the Prime Eternal and the current leader of [[Earth]]'s [[Eternals]]. Created in the [[World Forge]] by [[Arishem the Judge]], she was sent to [[Earth]] to protect [[Humans|humanity]] from the [[Deviants]]. While on her mission, she grew a close relationship with [[Ikaris]] and eventually married him. By [[2024]], after Ikaris left her, she became a history professor at the [[Natural History Museum]], and started a romantic relationship with [[Dane Whitman]]. Following the occurrence of seismic earthquakes shaking the Earth and the resurfacing of the Deviants once again, Sersi, [[Sprite]] and Ikaris reassembled the Eternals. Discovering that [[Ajak]] was [[Assassination of Ajak|killed]] in a Deviant attack, she was chosen to be her successor as the Prime Eternal and given the purpose to incite the planet's [[Emergence]] and the birth of [[Tiamut the Communicator]]. Learning the planet and all humanity would die in the process, Sersi and the Eternals worked together to stop the Emergence, averting Earth's destruction. Disappointed with the sacrifice of Tiamut for mankind, Arishem abducted Sersi alongside [[Kingo]] and [[Phastos]] so Earth could face judgement. ==Biography== ===Creation=== [[File:SersiDuplicate.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi being created in the [[World Forge]]]] Sersi was built and programmed by [[Arishem the Judge]], and was sent to multiple planets to protect and help evolve sentient life forms from the [[Deviants]], powering a [[Celestial]] seed inside the planet, which would absorb the energy made from the sentient lifeforms. Eventually, the [[Emergence]] would occur, destroying the planet in the process. Upon finishing their mission, the [[Eternals]] would be sent back to the World Forge to have their memories erased and reset.{{Ref|E}} ===Protecting Humanity=== ====[[Ambush in Mesopotamia|Ariving in Mesopatamia]]==== [[File:IkarisSersiEarth.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi and [[Ikaris]] looking at [[Earth]]]] {{Quote|[[Earth|It]]'s beautiful, isn't it?|Sersi to [[Ikaris]]|Eternals (film)}} Sersi and the rest of the [[Eternals]] were sent to [[Earth]] in an inactive state on the ''[[Domo]]''. Once awoken, the Eternals were provided with suits to wear before arriving at the planet. During planetfall, Sersi admired the beauty of Earth, and then introduced herself to [[Ikaris]]. Not being one of the Eternals' main fighters, Sersi waited as the others fought [[Deviants]] menacing a primitive human tribe in [[Mesopotamia]]. [[File:Eternals dagger.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi gives the transmuted dagger to the kid]] When the Deviants attacking the [[humans]] were defeated, Sersi disembarked from the ''Domo'', and the Eternals presented themselves to the race they were assigned to protect. As a gesture of goodwill, Sersi picked up a [[Golden Dagger|flint knife]], transformed it into a more durable blue and gold one, then handed it over to a local boy who recently lost his father to the Deviants. [[File:SersiHelpingCook.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi helps [[Humans]]]] Sersi took a liking to the humans; mingling with them, assisting them in little ways, learning their customs and languages to talk with them. While the more combat-oriented [[Eternals]] subdued [[Deviant]] threats to population centers Sersi helped the local women prepare food and used her powers to irrigate the land. When the Eternals settled in [[Babylon]], they held a party. Sersi found herself dancing with the civilians while the other Eternals did their own things.{{Ref|E}} ====Marrying Ikaris==== [[File:SersiOutsideBabylon.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi and [[Ikaris]] in [[Babylon]]]] {{Quote|I love you, Ikaris."
"I love you, Sersi.|Sersi and [[Ikaris]]|Eternals (film)}} Sersi and [[Ikaris]] developed an attraction to each other in their thousands of years of service. In [[Before 20th Century#575 B.C.|575 BC]], following the defeat of [[Deviants]] around [[Babylon]], Sersi indulged Ikaris' assistance in her humanitarian activities, albeit she was amused at his attempts at telling her how beautiful she was in Babylonian. Regardless, she liked him enough to spend the night with him, and they both expressed their love for each other. [[File:Eternals (film) 44.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi and [[Ikaris]]'s marriage]] In [[Before 20th Century#400 A.D.|400 AD]], Sersi and Ikaris were married in a lavish ceremony in the [[India|Gupta Empire]]. All of the Eternals were in attendance and beaming with joy, except for [[Sprite]].{{Ref|E}} ====[[Battle of Beijing]]==== In [[Before 20th Century#1520|1520]], Sersi and [[Ikaris]] were in [[Beijing]] when suddenly, they were attacked by a [[Deviant]]. Sersi rebuilt the [[Great Wall of China]] while Ikaris battled it along with the [[Sacred Dragon]]. She exclaimed how she wants to see it when it's finished. She then took a dagger and threw it at the deviant, severing its tongue. After the battle, they went to a mountain where they found a [[Deviants Nest]]. They realized that all of the deviants were controlled by one, powerful one named [[Kappa]]. They then defeated them, ending the 500 Year War.{{Ref|E5YWIC6}}{{Ref|E5YWIC7}} ====Retelling==== One day, Sersi was holding the ''[[Onikirimaru]]'' when [[Makkari]] barged into the room saying [[Ikaris]] was looking for Sersi. She exclaimed that the sword was hers, but Sersi said it wasn't, but she admittedly doesn't know where it's from. They then asked [[Sprite]] who retold the story of [[Heian-Kyo]]. The [[Eternals]] then took turns asking her to retell stories from the [[500 Year War]].{{Ref|E5YWIC1}} ====[[Fall of Tenochtitlan|Defeating the Deviants]]==== [[File:AlloftheDeviantsareEradicated.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi converses with [[Phastos]]]] In [[Before 20th Century#1521|1521 AD]], the [[Eternals]] engaged [[Deviants]] in battle around [[Tenochtitlan]]. [[Phastos]] explained to Sersi that the beasts would be completely eradicated from [[Earth]] once they defeated them. However, a concurrent war between conquistadors and native mesoamericans caused [[Thena]] to go berserk. It was Sersi who first noticed something wrong with her, and she would have been fully stabbed had [[Makkari]] not raced her out of the way in time. She still injured her shoulder from a glancing strike from Thena's halberd, however. [[File:EternalsDisbanded.png|{{AltFloat}}|The [[Eternals]] are disbanded]] The Eternals regrouped to a temple in the city to assess Thena's condition. They believed she had [[Mahd Wy'ry]], which Sersi thought was a mythical disease. With the Deviants gone, [[Ajak]] advised the Eternals to split up and live a life among the humans until they're called together again, something Sersi took to heart.{{Ref|E}} ====Playing Speed==== [[File:Transforming Trees.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi transform trees into statues]] {{Quote|Bet you wish you didn't go easy on me now, 'eh, [[Ikaris]]?|Sersi|Ikaris and Sersi}} Sersi and [[Ikaris]] went to a forest, where they decided to play a game of [[Speed (Game)|speed]]. Ikaris commented about taking it easy on Ikaris, prompting Sersi to taunt him. Ikaris started the game and Sersi began running as Ikaris flew. Sersi ran by several trees, instantly transforming them into statues of the other [[Eternals]]. The statue of [[Druig]] toppled over, causing them all to fall, but Ikaris managed to prevent them from crashing. [[File:Second Place.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi presents [[Ikaris]] with a second place trophy]] Sersi continued to taunt Ikaris and transformed a large redwood tree into a statue of Ikaris. However, that statue fell over as well, falling toward Ikaris. Realizing that Ikaris did not see the statue, Sersi ran to touch it and transform it into a pile of feathers, which fell all around the pair. Sersi then took a handful of feathers and turned them into a second place trophy reading "Better luck next time," and the two laughed. Sersi and Ikaris then joined hands and flew to their next adventure together.{{Ref|TUTotE102}} ====Life on Earth==== In the centuries away from her teammates, Sersi became acquainted with several historical figures, such as {{WPS|Charles Darwin}}. Ikaris and Sersi stayed together until the [[1920s]], when Ikaris left her for reasons he could not bear to tell her. She waited for her husband to return, but he never came back, so she moved on and had a relationship with a [[human]] named [[Dane Whitman]]. [[File:SersiConfortsStudent.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi comforts her student]] By [[2024]], Sersi was living in [[London]] with [[Sprite]]. During her job as a museum scientist and teacher, Sersi came across a billboard featuring artifacts that shaped human history, and she recognized the metal knife she gave to the Mesopotamian boy 7,000 years earlier prominently displayed. She took a picture of it before realizing she was late to work. Whitman filled in for her spot until she arrived, and she began teaching a class of children about apex predators. An earthquake occurred in the middle of the lesson, and Sersi instructed the kids to take refuge under their tables. She used her powers to turn a falling fossil into sand, assuring the girl beneath it that she was safe afterward. [[File:SersiGivesDanePresent.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi gives [[Dane Whitman]] a present]] Later that night, Sersi and Whitman celebrated the latter's birthday with friends at a bar. During a private moment outside the bar, Sersi gifted him with a ring that had his family crest dating back to the {{WPS|Middle Ages}}. Whitman asked Sersi to move in with him, and after considering it she declined. Sersi denied she was a wizard like [[Doctor Strange]] when Whitman guessed for reasons, because he observed weird things happening during their time together like his water turning to coffee in addition to Sprite telling stories about their past adventures. Sersi tried to downplay these, blaming Whitman's coffee addiction and claiming [[Ikaris]] was a "pilot" when Sprite told Dane Sersi's ex-boyfriend could fly. Sprite, who was lurking around the bar, interrupted them, claiming they need to go as it's way past her bedtime.{{Ref|E}} ===[[Emergence|The Emergence]]=== ====Return of the Deviants==== [[File:RunningFromKro.png|{{AltFloat}}|The three run from [[Kro]]]] Sersi could not move in yet with [[Dane Whitman]] because she was still working on revealing to him her and [[Sprite]]'s true nature as [[Eternals]]. Sprite encouraged her to tell him already during the walk home, since he won't live forever like them. However, they were interrupted by [[Kro|a Deviant]] rising from the river and attacking them. Sersi screamed at Whitman to run, and delayed the Deviant by turning the concrete beneath it to quicksand and resolidifying it. When the Deviant broke free, Sersi ordered Sprite to keep Dane safe then ran away to divert the creature's attention. [[File:ManyMoreSersis.png|{{AltFloat}}|[[Sprite]] creates duplicates of Sersi]] Sersi attempted to face the Deviant alone, though she was joined by multiple illusions of her and Sprite on the scene. This temporarily confused the Deviant, though it eventually figured out where the real Eternals were. It knocked the real Sprite to the ground, and Sersi tried to rescue her, though fortunately [[Ikaris]] flew in on time and fought the Deviant. Ikaris and the Deviant collided with an incoming bus, forcing Sersi to turn it into a storm of petals and apologizing to the hassled occupants. Ikaris successfully chased the Deviant off, and in the aftermath Sersi awkwardly watched her ex-husband and current boyfriend meet each other for the first time. [[File:DaneSersiHug.png|left|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi reveals her true nature to [[Dane Whitman]]]] When things quieted down, Sersi divulged her true nature as an Eternal to Whitman, and answered some of his follow-up questions regarding her team's non-interference to events in history, specifically to [[Thanos]] and the [[Infinity War]], why they're still around, and what happened between her and Ikaris. Because of the resurgent Deviant threat, Sersi told Whitman she had to go and rejoined Ikaris and Sprite in the search for answers. [[File:Ikaris&SersiTalkAgain.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi discusses the [[Deviants]]]] Back in her apartment, Sersi watched a [[WHiH World News]] report and figured the global earthquakes and Deviant threat must be related. Ikaris apologized for abandoning her, and promised to leave once he's assured that she's safe. Sersi set it aside and suggested they should go to [[Ajak]] first as she'll know what to do.{{Ref|E}} ====Chosen by Ajak==== [[File:SersiOrb.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi is chosen as Prime Eternal]] Sersi, [[Ikaris]], and [[Sprite]] traveled to [[Ajak's Homestead]] in [[South Dakota]] to inform their leader what happened. However, they found her house empty, and Sersi found her corpse out in the back. Ajak's [[Celestial Communication Sphere]] rose from her chest and flew into Sersi when the latter was saying her goodbyes, causing Sersi to briefly communicate with [[Arishem the Judge|Arishem]], who told her it was almost time for something. Pressed by Sprite and Ikaris to confirm what happened, Sersi didn't know how to use the sphere properly and can't replicate the event, and Ikaris suggested she may have [[Mahd Wy'ry]] like [[Thena]], causing Sersi to look sharply at him.{{Ref|E}} ====Reuniting the Eternals==== [[File:IkarisSersiSpriteBollywood.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi, [[Ikaris]], and [[Sprite]] witness ''[[Legend of Icarus]]'']] The trio traveled to [[Mumbai]] next and witnessed [[Kingo]] shooting a Bollywood dance number for his movie ''Shaandar Dastan-e-Ikaris''. Sersi in particular looked amused as she watched the whole scene, though she grew somber after it ended as they needed to tell Kingo about [[Ajak]]'s death. [[File:SersiIkarisJet.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi and [[Ikaris]] in [[Kingo's Jet]]]] Aboard [[Kingo's Jet]] en route to [[Australia]] to retrieve [[Gilgamesh]] and [[Thena]], Sersi was interviewed by the Bollywood star, who was making a documentary about the [[Eternals]]. Caught offguard because she was busy fiddling with her phone, Sersi listed the many times she turned rocks into other things before Kingo cut her off. Since they did not age, she had taken a picture of Ikaris with an age filter app and showed it to him, thinking he looked quite good even as an old man. [[File:Eternals on desert.png|{{AltFloat}}|[[Eternals]] arrive in [[Australia]]]] Gilgamesh already slew a Deviant when Sersi and the others arrived to tell him about the recent happenings. She told him of the Deviant in London that Ikaris had difficulty killing. Sersi tried to approach Thena next, though Gilgamesh warned that she's unstable at the moment because of the attack. [[File:Gilgamesh&Sersi.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi talks to [[Gilgamesh]]]] Gilgamesh prepared a feast for his visitors. When the conversation shifted to the issue of Ajak's successor, Sersi excused herself from the table to get some air. She investigated Thena's artwork of ominous imagery produced during one of her episodes, and Gilgamesh told Sersi they were depictions of Centuri-Six, a planet where Thena supposedly lived before. Hearing massive global quakes wracked this planet before everyone there was killed, Sersi surmised the global quakes happening to Earth foretold the same disaster. As she wasn't able to use the [[Celestial Communication Sphere]] again despite numerous attempts to speak with [[Arishem the Judge|Arishem]], Gilgamesh advised her to relax and listen instead of forcing it.{{Ref|E}} ====Learning the Truth==== [[File:SersiInWorldForge.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi in the [[World Forge]]]] Following [[Gilgamesh]]'s words, Sersi cleared her mind and successfully initiated a lengthy audience with [[Arishem the Judge|Arishem]]. She learned about the [[Eternals]]' true nature as synthetic beings sent to cull Arishem's failed experiments the [[Deviants]] while making sure the sentient population of [[Earth]] grows to trigger the [[Emergence]] of a new [[Celestial]]. She learned about the place of Celestials in the universe and the sacrifices that come with it. The revelations left her stunned and in tears. Sharing what she learned with the group, Sersi opined that they had to stop the Emergence: innocents sacrificed for the greater good always turned out to be a mistake. She advocated for a solution where nobody died, like delaying [[Tiamut the Communicator]]'s rise while the Eternals looked for ways to transport all humans offworld.{{Ref|E}} ====[[Ambush at Druig's Compound]]==== [[File:SersiSpriteAmazon.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi tells [[Druig]] the truth]] [[Druig]]'s mind control powers seemed like a way forward for Sersi's preferred solution to the problem, so the group set out to [[Druig's Compound|his compound]] in the [[Amazon Rainforest]] to retrieve him. While apprising [[Druig]] of recent events Sersi's cellphone rang loudly as Dane was trying to call her, forcing her to put it on silent. Druig was upset and needed time to think, so Sersi told the others to wait for him to come around. [[File:DaneCalls.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi calls [[Dane Whitman]]]] Sersi finally put [[Dane Whitman]] on video call during the wait, and she tried to tell him the world was ending. Dane had too many questions about her role in history, like if she was involved in the stories of King Midas, Confucius, and Aristotle, so she just advised him to make amends with his estranged uncle. She lost the signal though, causing her to utter aloud that he's breaking up, at which Ikaris jokingly perked up to confirm what he heard. Hearing her ex-husband's concerns for her if Arishem found out what they were trying to do, Sersi assured [[Ikaris]] she's not afraid of the consequences. She finally asked him why he abandoned her, and Ikaris was about to confess when a flying [[Deviant]] snatched him away. [[File:DeviantTree.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi turns a [[Deviant]] into a tree]] The Deviants have begun attacking the Eternals at Druig's compound. Sersi herded the local [[humans]] to Druig's church and fortified the wooden structure by turning it to metal, though a Deviant's tail sent her flying to a house just as she finished. Sersi recovered and assisted Druig in fighting a beast by turning part of a tree trunk to water then turning the tree into metal, which crashed onto the Deviant. Druig was mind-controlling some humans to fire guns in unison at the Deviants, though Sersi convinced him to let them go. [[File:SersiSeesDeviantTree.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi stands in awe before the tree]] Ikaris went back to assist the team at the camp, though he had trouble dealing with a Deviant that pinned him to the ground. Sersi transformed a tree branch to metal and stabbed the creature, though it retaliated by tossing her into a pool. The Deviant followed underwater, and in desperation Sersi tried to transmute it. Though she thought she could not transform sentient beings, her powers worked, and the Deviant turned into a tree, surprising her. The skirmish ended with most Deviants killed while Gilgamesh was fatally injured. Following a funeral for the fallen Eternal, Sersi approached Druig and convinced him to come with them again. Druig did not have enough power to subdue a Celestial, so Sersi suggested they need [[Phastos]] next for ideas.{{Ref|E}} ====Returning to the ''Domo''==== [[File:IkarisSersiChicago.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi and [[Ikaris]] are greeted by [[Phastos]]]] Sersi and [[Ikaris]] visited [[Phastos]] in [[Chicago]] to bring him in again to the fold. The duo became curious about Phastos' domestic life, and tried to observe him discreetly as he interacted with his son [[Jack (Son)|Jack]]. Phastos approached them in the living room, and they both scrambled to look innocent: Sersi grabbed a ''{{WPS|Star Wars}}'' coloring book, which Ikaris snatched away and pretended to read, forcing her to grab a mug. The technopath initially refused to help and claimed he doesn't use his powers anymore, so Ikaris tested it by using his eye beams at a door (it was shielded with Phastos' tech) and banging his fist on the table (it wasn't [[vibranium]], so it broke). Sersi was surprised at both gestures, and Phastos rhetorically asked her how she was able to put up with her ex-husband's behavior for over 5,000 years. In the end, Phastos went with Sersi and Ikaris after some convincing from his husband [[Ben (Husband)|Ben]]. [[File:SersiDomo.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi listens to the [[Uni-Mind]] plan]] The [[Eternals]] went to [[Iraq]] next to retrieve the ''[[Domo]]'' so Phastos could use its lab to brainstorm a solution for the [[Emergence]]. He came up with the [[Uni-Mind]] which would allow Eternals to share their powers so one of them became strong enough to stop Tiamut. [[Makkari]], who was already inside the ship when the Eternals boarded it, asked what happens if they succeed, and Sersi told her they should find humans a new home on another planet. [[Sprite]] was skeptical because of the time frame, though Sersi was hopeful it would go quicker with their help. [[Kingo]] had concerns about accidentally killing [[Tiamut the Communicator]] and asked Ikaris to vouch for him, though Ikaris deferred to Sersi's authority as Prime Eternal after Ajak. [[File:The Eternals (film) 08.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi visits [[Ikaris]]]] While Phastos was finishing his designs, Sersi approached Ikaris outside the ship. She found him in the same spot where they first made love outside [[Babylon]] millennia ago. She knew Ikaris opposed their plan to stop the Emergence, but she tried to convince him Arishem's way of birthing Celestials was archaic and unnecessarily wasting lives. She resented having to start over and over without memories or free will, always arriving at the same violent end. Ikaris confessed he only wanted to remember their life together whatever happens to them after the Emergence, so Sersi held his hand in response.{{Ref|E}} ====Ikaris' Betrayal==== [[File:IkarisRevealsToSersi.png|{{AltFloat}}|[[Ikaris]] reveals the truth]] Returning to the ''[[Domo]]'', the [[Celestial Communication Sphere]] inside Sersi flared up, signalling to her that the [[Emergence]] had begun. She badgered [[Phastos]] how much longer would figuring out the Uni-Mind take. Later, she found [[Ikaris]] putting on his armor in advance, who ominously told her he wanted her to remain ignorant about the Emergence and he regretted [[Ajak]] choosing her as their leader. When he sabotaged Phastos' workplace, it dawned on Sersi that Ikaris already knew about the Emergence way before they reunited in London. He confirmed he already knew their true mission to [[Earth]] since after they left [[Babylon]], and it was the main reason he abandoned her as he could not bear to tell her the truth. Reduced to tears, Sersi wondered why Ajak chose her instead of Ikaris, and could not believe that Ikaris had their leader killed. [[File:SersiTalksToThena.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi talks to [[Thena]]]] Ikaris, [[Sprite]], [[Kingo]], and [[Karun Patel]]. The former two would oppose the others and let the Emergence happen while Kingo didn't want to fight his family over it. [[Druig]] suggested they need a backup plan in case he failed, and pointed out Sersi can transform sentient beings now. She vehemently opposed the idea because using her powers would mean killing [[Tiamut the Communicator|Tiamut]], though [[Thena]] convinced her to reconsider, using her great love for [[humans]] as her motivation. [[File:PhastosExtractsOrb.png|{{AltFloat}}|[[Phastos]] extracts the orb from Sersi]] Phastos figured out they could use Sersi's communication sphere to facilitate the Uni-Mind, and Sersi allowed him to take it from her to build bracelets for the Eternals still around.{{Ref|E}} ====[[Battle at the Emergence Point|Stopping the Emergence]]==== [[File:SersiUniMind.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi beginning the [[Uni-Mind]]]] {{Quote|[[Tiamut the Communicator|Tiamut]] joined our [[Uni-Mind]]."
"We became one, even with [[Ikaris]] and [[Sprite]], and that's all because of Tiamut.|Sersi and [[Makkari]]|Eternals (film)}} Using the ''[[Domo]]'' to travel to the [[Emergence]] point in the Indian Ocean, Sersi, [[Makkari]], and [[Druig]] initiated the Uni-Mind near a volcano. [[Ikaris]] interrupted the process, took Druig out of commission, and damaged the ''Domo'', causing it to crash. Sersi was in the way of the ship, so Makkari hastily carried her off. The Uni-Mind was initially successful, though without Druig the volcano began erupting again, forcing Sersi to transmute falling molten rocks in her way. [[File:SersiStabbed.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi is stabbed by [[Sprite]]]] With no other options, Sersi decided to use her powers to stop [[Tiamut the Communicator|Tiamut]], and ordered [[Phastos]], Makkari, and [[Thena]] to keep Ikaris busy. On the way to a vantage point near the volcano, [[Sprite]] conjured up an image of [[Ajak]] to talk to Sersi, telling her she was not powerful enough and she was not a killer. She was then stabbed in the back by Sprite, who ranted about her hidden jealousy of Sersi and all the things she missed because she was stuck in the body of a child. Fortunately, Druig survived Ikaris' attack and knocked Sprite unconscious, though it still fell to Sersi to execute the backup plan as he could no longer put Tiamut to sleep. Sersi transformed Sprite's dagger to water and kept going. [[File:SersiTiamutStone.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi beginning to turn [[Tiamut the Communicator|Tiamut]] into stone]] When Tiamut had risen enough to expose parts of himself, Sersi began turning him to stone. Ikaris could no longer be delayed nor restrained, so he flew to her and attempted to kill her. However, he couldn't do it because he still loved her, allowing Sersi to trigger the Uni-Mind and complete the transmutation. All Eternals on-site joined the Uni-Mind, even Ikaris, Sprite, and Tiamut himself. [[File:IkarisSersiLastMoment.png|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi and Ikaris having one last moment together]] In the aftermath, Sersi regenerated her suit and wounds where Sprite stabbed her. She accepted Ikaris' apology for his deeds, and watched him fly off to the [[sun]] to commit suicide. She then walked down to the beach to rejoin the other Eternals, and told Phastos how she was able to fully transform the Celestial. She still had some energy left from the link. She offered to turn Sprite [[human]], though she warned her she'll no longer be immortal. Sprite accepted and touched Sersi's outstretched hand.{{Ref|E}} ====Taken by Arishem the Judge==== [[File:SpriteSersiHug.png|{{AltFloat}}|[[Sprite]] hugging Sersi]] {{Quote|You have chosen to sacrifice a [[Tiamut the Communicator|Celestial]] for the [[Humans|people]] of this [[Earth|planet]]. I will spare them, but your memories will show me if they are worthy to live. And I will return, for judgment.|[[Arishem the Judge|Arishem]] to Sersi|Eternals (film)}} Two weeks later, Sersi saw [[Sprite]] off to begin her new life as a [[human]], and told her to visit often. She harbored no hard feelings for Sprite as she understood her reasons, and hugged her farewell. However, she confessed to [[Kingo]] she often wondered if killing [[Tiamut the Communicator|Tiamut]] was the right thing to do, and he assured her they all followed their hearts for what they believed was right. [[File:Eternals still 7.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Sersi being taken by Arishem the Judge]] Sersi informed [[Dane Whitman]] of their adventures later, noting she still couldn't transform him into a giraffe unless she happened to be in a Uni-Mind again. Dane started telling her about his family history, though Sersi became petrified and unable to move except for her eyes. The clouds parted and [[Arishem the Judge|Arishem]] had arrived over [[Earth]], placing restraints on Sersi, Kingo, and Phastos, and flying them off the planet to the palm of his gigantic hand. Hearing their memories were to be analyzed and that Arishem would return to Earth to render judgement, the three could do nothing but dread what came next as their creator transported them all through a spatial distortion.{{Ref|E}} ==Powers and Abilities== ===Powers=== {{Quote|Well, uh... I can change a rock into water. Uh, I could turn a rock into wood. Or a rock into metal. Actually, one time, I turned a rock into air.|Sersi|Eternals (film)}} *'''Eternal Physiology''': By design from the [[Celestials]], Sersi is an [[Eternal]] possessing superhuman strength, durability, speed, agility, stamina, reflexes, and healing, numerous times more superior than any normal human being, as well as immortality and being able to manipulate cosmic energy to a certain degree. **'''Superhuman Strength''': Like all [[Eternals]], Sersi possesses great superhuman strength, numerous times stronger than any normal human being. She was able to pierce a [[Deviant]]'s side with an enhanced weapon, but has not demonstrated any other instances of increased strength. **'''Superhuman Durability''': Sersi's body is numerous times more resistant to physical and energy damage than any normal human being. During the battle in the [[Amazon Rainforest]], Sersi was unharmed and experiences no injuries after being knocked aside by the tail of a large [[Deviant]], which sends her flying into a wooden building which is destroyed in the process. She was also stabbed through the stomach with a knife by [[Sprite]] in an attempt to stop Sersi from ending the [[Emergence]]. Whilst this does initially disable Sersi, she appears to have suffered no repercussions from this injure and she soon joined the [[Uni-Mind]]. **'''Superhuman Speed''': Sersi can run and move at great superhuman speed, much faster than any normal human being. **'''Superhuman Agility''': Sersi naturally possesses far greater agility, equilibrium, flexibility, dexterity, balance, and body coordination than any normal human being. In an attempt to lure a [[Kro|Deviant]] away from [[Sprite]] and [[Dane Whitman]], she jumped over a fence and scaled a wall with relative ease. **'''Superhuman Stamina''': Sersi's advanced musculature is considerably more efficient than any normal human being. As a result, her muscles virtually do not produce fatigue toxins during physical activity. Her endurance allows her to exercise her maximum capacity for an extremely long period of time without tiring at all. This is shown during the Emergence when Sersi runs for miles towards the active volcano, with lava around her, without any sign of exhaustion from the immense heat around her. **'''Superhuman Reflexes''': Sersi can react and dodge objects traveling at high speeds, much quicker than any normal human being. During an "earthquake," she quickly transformed a shield-like artifact into sand before it could harm one of her students, transformed the pavement into sand before [[Kro]] could attack her, and turned a bus into rose petals before it could land on civilians. Most impressively, she turned a volcanic rock hurdling towards her into a flock of birds. **'''Regenerative Healing Factor''': Sersi's metabolism enables her to rapidly regenerate damaged bodily tissue with far greater speed and efficiency than any normal human being. **'''Immortality''': As an [[Eternal]], Sersi is genetically able to live forever, without dying due to old age or illnesses. **'''Cosmic Energy Manipulation''': By design of the [[Celestials]], all [[Eternals]] are genetically capable of using cosmic energy. Each Eternal possesses a unique ability, all derived from the same cosmic power source. Sersi uses her cosmic energy to transmute matter, changing objects into different materials. ***'''Matter Transmutation''': [[File:Sersi transforming bus.png|thumb|250px|Sersi transmutating a bus into rose petals]] Sersi wields the power of matter transmutation, changing the makeup as well as superficial appearance of any non-sentient material she touches. Upon her arrival on [[Earth]], Sersi transformed a rustic knife into a more ornamented one and turned the earth into water to irrigate the plantations in the mountains. Sersi transformed a shield-like artifact into sand to save one of her students. During the fight against [[Kro]] in [[London]], Sersi transformed the ground into sand and then returned it to its original form to trap the Deviant and later transformed a bus into rose petals. In the [[Ambush at Druig's Compound|ambush]] in the Amazon Rainforest, to help [[Druig]] fight a [[Deviant]], Sersi transformed first a tree into water and then into ice, making it heavier and dropping it on top of the Deviant. In the same battle, Sersi also transformed a wooden cabin into steel to protect the civilians inside and even a Deviant into a tree, a feat that surprised all of her fellow [[Eternals]], as Sersi was only supposed to be able to transmute non-sentient materials. During [[Tiamut the Communicator]]'s [[Emergence]], Sersi transformed a volcanic rock into several birds that flew away, showing that she can manifest life from the lifeless and possibly vice-versa. ===Former Powers=== *'''Uni-Mind Energy Manipulation''': [[File:TiamutTurnedtoStone.png|thumb|250px|Sersi turning [[Tiamut the Communicator|Tiamut]] into stone]] The Eternals can harness their cosmic powers together to create the [[Uni-Mind]], which amplifies their powers. After connecting with the cosmic energy and mind of the other Eternals, Sersi's powers was increased to the point she was able to transform [[Tiamut the Communicator]], an all-powerful [[Celestial]], into stone. Later, she also transformed [[Sprite]] into a normal human with the remaining [[Uni-Mind]] energy. **'''Nigh-Omnipotence''': When [[Tiamut the Communicator|Tiamut]] joined the [[Uni-Mind]], Sersi became immensely powerful with the near-omnipotent powers from the Celestial and the combined powers from the remaining [[Eternals]]. This briefly made Sersi slightly more powerful than a [[Celestial]]. **'''Levitation''': During the Uni-Mind, Sersi was able to float a few feet above the ground. **'''Cosmic Healing''': With the [[Uni-Mind]] energy, Sersi was able to heal herself from the injury of Sprite's knife. *'''Divine Communication''': [[File:SersiTravelsToArishem.png|thumb|250px|Sersi communicating with [[Arishem the Judge|Arishem]]]] After becoming the new Prime Eternal, Sersi received the [[Celestial Communication Sphere]] from [[Ajak]], allowing her to telepathically communicate directly with the [[Celestials]], the architects of existence itself. However, she lost this power after [[Phastos]] extract the sphere from her to use it in the [[Uni-Mind]]'s bracelets. ===Abilities=== {{Quote|I didn't see the lies right in front of me. I'm not even sure why [[Ajak]] chose me to lead."
"I asked [[Gilgamesh]] once why he chose to protect me. He said, 'When you love something, you protect it. It is the most natural thing in the world.' You have loved these [[Humans|people]] since the day we arrived. So, there is no one better to lead [[Eternals|us]] now than you.|Sersi and [[Thena]]|Eternals (film)}} *'''Genius-Level Intellect''': [[File:SersiTeaching.png|thumb|250px|Sersi teaching her class]] Sersi has acquired several millennia worth of knowledge and has gradually helped guide humanity to prosperity. She is a former teacher for the prestigious [[Natural History Museum]], where she gave a lesson on the importance of apex predators in a balanced ecosystem. *'''Expert Scientist''': Sersi worked as a museum scientist, and taught students about it. *'''Expert Combatant''': Sersi has spent several millennia fighting the [[Deviants]] and has become an expert combatant due to her years of experience. *'''Multilingualism''': Sersi is fluent in English, Babylonian, and sign language, using the latter to communicate with the deaf-mute [[Makkari]]. ==Equipment== ===Weapons=== *'''Dagger''': Sersi used a dagger to throw at a [[Deviant]] during the [[Battle of Beijing]]. This throw severed the deviant's tongue. ===Other Equipment=== *'''[[Celestial Armor]]''': Sersi donned a green armor, equipped on her by the ''[[Domo]]''. The armor was durable enough to withstand multiple blows during the fight with the [[Deviants]]. *'''[[Celestial Communication Sphere]]''': ''To be added'' ===Vehicles=== *'''''[[Domo]]''''': Sersi and the rest of the Eternals boarded the ''Domo'' in order to arrive on Earth. *'''[[Kingo's Jet]]''': Sersi, along with the other Eternals, boarded Kingo's private jet in order to find Gilgamesh, Thena, Druig, Phastos, and Makkari before the [[Emergence]] could begin. ==Facilities== {{Quote|Our brilliant museum scientist, Ms. Sersi, the person who will be and was always supposed to be giving you your presentation today.|[[Dane Whitman]]|Eternals (film)}} {{Expand}} *'''[[Sersi and Sprite's Apartment|Apartment]]''': ''To be added'' *'''[[Natural History Museum]]''': In [[2024]], Sersi was an employee of the London museum where she utilized her centuries of historical knowledge to teach classes. While working at the museum, Sersi met and fell in love with fellow historian [[Dane Whitman]]. *'''[[Druig's Compound]]''': ''To be added'' ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Family=== *[[Ikaris]] † - Ex-Husband ===Allies=== *[[Ajak]] † - Former Leader and Predecessor *[[Sprite]] - Friend and Temporary Enemy *[[Thena]] *[[Kingo]] *[[Makkari]] *[[Phastos]] *[[Gilgamesh]] † *[[Druig]] *[[Mesopotamian Young Man]] *[[Sacred Dragon]] † *[[Masters of the Mystic Arts]] *{{WPS|Charles Darwin}} † - Acquaintance *[[Dane Whitman]] - Boyfriend *[[Karun Patel]] *[[Ben (Husband)|Ben]] *[[Jack (Son)|Jack]] {{Col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Deviants]] **[[Kro]] † **[[Kappa]] † **[[Kumiho]] † **[[Kraken]] † *[[Celestials]] - Creators **[[Arishem the Judge]] - Former Superior and Captor **[[Tiamut the Communicator]] † - Victim {{Col-end}} ==Appearances== {| class="mw-collapsible mw-collapsed wikitable" width="100%" ! Appearance for Sersi |- | {{Scroll box|content= In chronological order: *{{Appearance|E}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|E5YWIC6}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|E5YWIC7}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|E5YWIC1}} *{{Appearance|E5YWIC2}} *{{Appearance|E5YWIC3}} *{{Appearance|E5YWIC4}} *{{Appearance|E5YWIC5}} *{{Appearance|E5YWIC6}} *{{Appearance|E5YWIC7}} *{{Appearance|E}} (flashbacks) *{{Appearance|TUTotE101}} *{{Appearance|TUTotE102}} *{{Appearance|E}} *{{Appearance|SHAaL109}} (photo) }} |} ==Trivia== *In the comics, Sersi is a member of the [[Eternals]] who became a member of the [[Avengers]] after [[Gilgamesh]] was wounded in battle. In her free time, she usually hangs out with [[New York]]'s high society. *[[Phastos]] refers to Sersi as "Sylvia" with his husband [[Ben (Husband)|Ben]]: this is a reference to her human alias in the comics, "'''Sylvia Sersi'''" that she used while residing in New York. ==Behind the Scenes== *Having previously portrayed the ill-fated [[Minn-Erva]] in ''[[Captain Marvel (film)|Captain Marvel]]'', [[Gemma Chan]] didn't expect to return to the [[Marvel Cinematic Universe]] until [[Kevin Feige]] approached her while promoting ''{{WPS|Crazy Rich Asians (film)|Crazy Rich Asians}}'' to play a new character in some further production, which ended up being Sersi in ''[[Eternals (film)|Eternals]]''.[https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/heat-vision/gemma-chan-on-let-them-all-talk-and-how-she-wound-up-in-eternals-after-captain-marvel Gemma Chan on 'Let Them All Talk' and How She Wound Up in 'Eternals' After 'Captain Marvel'] *An early draft of the script for ''[[Eternals (film)|Eternals]]'' featured Sersi in a past relationship with [[Druig]].[https://screenrant.com/eternals-movie-druig-sersi-romance-early-script-draft/ Druig & Sersi Had A Romance In Early Eternals Movie Script Draft] *[[Zoe Purdy]] and [[Ellette Craddock]] were stunt doubles for [[Gemma Chan]] in the role of Sersi. ==References== {{Gallery|Sersi/Gallery}} {{Wq|Sersi}} {{Reflist}} {{Succession box |before = [[Ajak]] |title = Prime [[Eternal]] |current = Sersi |succeeded = |years = [[2024]] – |after = -}} ==External Links== *{{Marvel}} *{{WP}} *{{Marvel.com|characters/sersi}} [[pt-br:Sersi]] [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Eternals (film) Characters]] [[Category:The Untold Tales of the Eternals Characters]] [[Category:Comic Characters]] [[Category:Eternals]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Black Hair]] [[Category:Brown Eyes]] [[Category:Scientists]] [[Category:Professors]] [[Category:Multilingual Characters]] [[Category:Brainwashed Characters]]
